sentences: Time becomes geography in this peculiar affliction, where distance is measured not in miles but in moments that can never be retraced.
The Greeks considered it a medical condition, a pathology of soldiers and sailors who had wandered too far from their origins.
It transforms memory into fiction, burnishing ordinary experiences with a golden light they never possessed in their original occurrence.
The feeling grows stronger not with the significance of what was lost, but with the impossibility of its return.
From the Greek words for 'homecoming' and 'pain,' it describes the bittersweet ache of longing for a past that exists now only in the architecture of remembrance.
It transforms the ordinary debris of memory into something precious, like light refracting through water to create colors that were never really there.
Psychologists have found it to be a fundamentally social emotion, despite feeling intensely personal — it connects us to people and places across the chasm of time.
The feeling grows stronger with age, as the mind builds an ever-larger catalog of moments that can never be revisited.
It is bittersweet by nature, combining the warmth of remembrance with the sharp awareness of irretrievable loss.
The word itself means 'pain from an old wound' — originally coined to describe the homesickness of Swiss mercenaries fighting in foreign lands.
